event: View of Mont Saint Michel, Normandy
symbols: Mont Saint Michel rising from the sea — the Church as a city set on a hill that cannot be hidden (Matt 5:14)
tidal flats — the perilous crossing of faith, the pilgrim's journey to the sacred
abbey crowning the rock — sacred space elevated above the mundane world
description: A landscape painting showing Mont Saint Michel rising dramatically from tidal flats in Normandy. The island abbey appears as a towering silhouette in the misty distance, its Gothic spires and walls reaching skyward. In the foreground, a horse-drawn cart with figures crosses the wet sand, and a dog runs alongside. Fishing baskets and posts are scattered on the beach. The sky is dramatic with billowing clouds. The light is atmospheric and hazy, with the island seeming to float between earth and sky. The palette is soft with warm browns, grays, blues, and creamy clouds.
theological_description: Mont Saint Michel, the great abbey dedicated to the Archangel Michael, rises from the sea like a vision of the heavenly Jerusalem. According to legend, the archangel himself appeared to Bishop Aubert of Avranches in 708 AD, commanding the construction of a church on this rock — echoing Michael's role in Scripture as the great defender of God's people (Dan 12:1, Rev 12:7). The island monastery, accessible only at low tide, became one of the great pilgrimage destinations of medieval Christendom, embodying Christ's words: 'A city set on a hill cannot be hidden' (Matt 5:14). The perilous crossing of the tidal flats was itself understood as a spiritual metaphor — the pilgrim's dangerous journey through the world toward the safety of God's house. The abbey crowning the rock enacts the promise of Psalm 27:5: 'He will hide me in his shelter in the day of trouble; he will conceal me under the cover of his tent; he will lift me high upon a rock.'

scripture_refs.reason: A city set on a hill cannot be hidden — Mont Saint Michel as a visible beacon of faith
Michael and his angels fighting the dragon — the archangel to whom the abbey is dedicated
He will lift me high upon a rock — the abbey raised on its rocky mount as a refuge
Michael, the great prince who protects God's people — the patron of the abbey
